content:
Can you carify what you mean by "nothing is going into any of the sent folders that I have sent"?

A. You're not receiving any emails for a particular account. B. You're not receiving emails which you expect to arrive/show in a folder other than "Inbox". C. After sending an email, it is not being moved to the "sent mail" folder.

For point B, you need to enable sync on any custom folders you have. To do this...

1. Open the account list. 2. Select the applicable account. 3. Press "manage folders" (bottom of list). 4. Tap desired folder. 5. Toggle "sync" on.

Repeat for each required folder.

To manually sync accounts ... 1. Open the account list. 2. Tap the down arrow. 3. Tap the "Sync all accounts" button.

Please also ensure the app is excluded from any android battery optimisation and allowed to run in the background.

1. Long press the Thunderbird launcher icon. 2. Select "App Info" 3. Tap battery usage. 4. Toggle "Allow background activity" to ON
creator: platform34

content:
No problem ..

A refresh is performed by pressing the "sync all accounts" button as described in my previous reply. I've attached a screen shot for reference.

In regard to emails not being moved to your sent items folder...

1a. Navigate to: settings > account > sending mail 1b. Check "Upload sent messages" (to sent folder after sending) is ticked.

2a. Navigate to: settings > account > folders. 2b. Check "Sent folder" has a folder name assigned, usually it should say Automatic (sent). 2c. Set the folder if it shows a value = none (not assigned).

Delayed email fetching is usually due to the app not having permission to run in the background, hence the steps suggested in the previous reply.

You can also enable the "push" service which delivers emails immediately, rather than syncing every 15 minutes. See the guide linked below.

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/configure-push-email-thunderbird-android
creator: platform34
